body:
I only stumbled upon this problem recently - if I am not diligent about cleaning up my checkpointer files, the model may pickup older checkpoint files (which have a higher iteration number but were created a long time ago) without my realising. 

Obviously we can't foolproof everything, but this particular issue is insidious because it is completely silent, and thus may easily go unnoticed. To make it more obvious to the end-user what checkpoint file is being picked up, I have added an info statement with the file name and time created that is being picked up. This will assist in understanding what is happening a bit more. Keen to add if people think it is useful!

labels.description: Concerning a change which breaks the API
labels.name: breaking change :broken_heart:

title: (0.106) Log checkpoint file and mtime when restoring simulations

title: Fix typo with density perturbation in docs

body:
Unfortunately, setting wind stress and heat flux using ```FluxBoundaryCondition``` does not modify values inside the modeling domain when running on the Metal GPU. This behavior seems to be the result of materialization of the interior (```function maybe_copy_interior``` in ```OceananigansMetalExt.jl```), which was introduced in PR #4990 to enable reduction operators on the Metal GPU.

It appears that reduction operators can be enabled without materializing the interior. This PR is an attempt to enable reduction operators on the Metal GPU.
title: Rework support for reduction operations on Metal GPU to avoid materialization of the interior
